content.boundary: Applies to approval decisions for new or expanded 6/8-year gimnázium tracks across all maintainer types (state, church, foundation, private); does not alter existing already-approved tracks' admission criteria or capacity, and does not require primary legislation if implemented via ministerial regulation under existing discretionary authority.
content.change_lever: Formalization of a maintainer-neutral administrative segregation-impact test that must be satisfied before any new or expanded 6/8-year gimnázium track (state, church, foundation, or private) is approved, building on existing discretionary refusal precedent.
content.name: Targeted administrative constraint on segregation-generating track expansion (discretionary gatekeeping across all maintainer types)
content.system_problem: Track expansion decisions (e.g., adding grades 7-8 to an existing school) can reshape feeder-school composition through creaming effects at the local level, and this segregation-generating expansion channel operates across state, church, and private maintainers, but current discretionary refusal precedent has only been exercised inconsistently and does not yet constitute a formalized, maintainer-neutral policy.
